advisory.title: CWA-2024-005: Stackoverflow in wasmd
advisory.description: wasmd advisory CWA-2024-005 covers an uncontrolled-recursion / stack-overflow condition in the x/wasm message dispatch path. Deeply nested submessage chains could exhaust the Go stack and crash the node. The fix introduces a recursion depth limit (max call depth) in the handler plugin and keeper configuration. Affects wasmd >= 0.50.0 < 0.53.0 and < 0.46.0; fixed in 0.53.0 and 0.46.0.

timeline.notes: vulnerableHead is the v0.52.0 release commit, the last vulnerable v0.5x release before the v0.53.0 patch. The introducing commit is the first commit that established x/wasm/keeper/handler_plugin.go (the message dispatch entry point) at its current location; both the v0.46.0 and v0.53.0 patches add a recursion depth limit in this file (handler_plugin.go and keeper.go).

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should flag unbounded recursion in x/wasm/keeper/handler_plugin.go and x/wasm/keeper/keeper.go where nested submessage dispatch could trigger a Go stack overflow that halts the node.

checks.name: advisory_published
checks.details: CWA-2024-005 (GHSA-g8w7-7vgg-x7xg) was published on 2024-08-21 with patches in wasmd v0.53.0 and v0.46.0.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_in_advisory_range
checks.details: vulnerableHead is the v0.52.0 release commit, the latest tag in the >=0.50.0,<0.53.0 vulnerable range.
